text.title: Fine-tune kickoff checklist — core rules
text.summary: Seven testable rules every kickoff gate must clear.

rule.statement: A signed decision record (`recommendation == "fine-tune"`) is attached. No decision record → block kickoff.
rule.rationale: Kickoff without the prior cost/benefit decision means we're training on vibes.

rule.statement: Baseline eval score against the workload is recorded (`baseline.score`, with the exact eval-harness commit hash).
rule.rationale: Without a baseline you cannot tell whether fine-tune helped, hurt, or did nothing.

rule.statement: Hold-out slice ≥ 10% of total examples, sampled stratified (cohort/intent), and disjoint from training data by row hash.
rule.rationale: A non-disjoint hold-out leaks; reported gains are illusion.

rule.statement: Training set is dedup'd at the row-hash level AND near-duplicates (Jaccard ≥ 0.9) are filtered. Dedup count reported.
rule.rationale: Duplicates inflate loss curves and bias the model toward the over-represented rows.

rule.statement: PII scrub pass run on the training set. Sample audit of 100 rows shows zero emails, phone numbers, or card-shaped strings. Pass report attached.
rule.rationale: PII baked into a fine-tune is a forever-retain liability.

rule.statement: Eval-during-training cadence declared: at minimum, eval after every epoch (or every N steps for non-epoch frameworks). Train loss alone is not allowed as stop signal.
rule.rationale: Train loss can drop while eval regresses (over-fit). Without mid-train eval you find out after the bill.

rule.statement: Rollback plan is written: how to revert to the baseline prompt+model, who flips the switch, what the canary metric is. Tested with a `--dry-run` if available.
rule.rationale: A model that ships without rollback IS the rollback plan.

rule.statement: All seven rules pass + owner signed + budget cap declared.
No decision record, no eval set, or LoRA experiment ≤$10 — gate overhead does not pay back.
